title: lobelia puberula var. subglabra
intro: Lobelia puberula var. subglabra, also known as Indian tobacco or wild tobacco, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is rich in alkaloids, particularly lobeline, which has been shown to have health benefits including reducing nicotine cravings and improving cognitive function. Lobelia puberula var. subglabra can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and smoked preparations, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
